design_philosophy.core_loop: Run, jump, shoot at enemies across six varied stages (four side-scrolling, two overhead Mode 7 perspective). Swap between dual machine guns, collect power-ups to change shot types, use limited bombs strategically, defeat bosses at stage ends.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (limited bombs, power-up loss on death) combined with pattern recognition for boss patterns and enemy placements. Execution difficulty on later stages and hard mode.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Elite commando (Bill Rizer/Lance Bean) fighting alien invasion across Earth with escalating weapon variety and environmental challenges. Cinematic action-movie heroism with comedic tone.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Director Nobuya Nakazato intentionally shifted Contra from 'too realistic' action to include comedic elements (hanging from flying missiles). Demanded cinematic, progressive soundtrack. Prioritized 'tighter stage design' over predecessors. Embraced technical showmanship (raster scrolling tricks, Mode 7 overhead stages) to justify SNES hardware. Regional censorship (EU robots vs. human characters) reflects firm stance on violence content.

difficulty.lives_system: Player loses a life by touching enemies, enemy bullets, or falling into pits. Power-ups from equipped gun are lost upon death.
difficulty.continues_system: Japanese version features unlimited continues. International (US/EU) versions have limited continues, making them significantly harder.
difficulty.checkpointing: No traditional checkpoints documented. Stage-based progression with mid-stage bosses in some levels.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three selectable difficulty modes (Easy/Normal/Hard) with different final boss availability. Easy mode does not feature true final boss; Normal and Hard modes unlock additional content. Game Genie codes allow cheats (30 lives, sound test, level select) in international versions, suggesting developer acknowledgment of difficulty scaling needs.

progression.upgrade_system: Power-up based weapon upgrades dropped from flying pods. Six interchangeable shot types: homing missiles, torpedoes, flamethrower, laser, spread shot. Bombs function as consumable special attacks affecting entire screen. Dual machine guns can be swapped at will; picking up power-up replaces current gun's shot type.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Difficulty-gated content: Easy mode ends without true final boss with message 'Challenge yourself in a higher class mode.' Normal mode reveals additional narrative closure. Hard mode unlocks true final boss encounter and true ending with character celebration sequences.
replayability_hooks: Three difficulty modes with progressively harder bosses and final boss unlock on Hard
Regional differences (Japan/US/EU character variations and ending scenes)
Hidden level select and sound test in Japanese version
Varied stage design (4 side-scrolling, 2 overhead Mode 7 perspectives with unique controls per stage)
Multiple weapon types encourage different tactical approaches

world_building_techniques: Opening cinematic: Nakazato emphasized 'low-budget movie theme' with scrolling intro text ('Now, Contra's greatest battle begins' in US vs. 'Today, Contra's greatest battle begins' in JP)
Cutscenes between stages showing character progression and alien threat escalation
Difficulty-gated ending messages: Easy ('true big boss lurking somewhere'), Normal ('humanity's greatest crisis defeated, battle continues'), Hard ('achieved perfect victory, real hero')
Regional character replacement (robots in EU 'Super Probotector' to circumvent German violence laws) signals geopolitical worldbuilding
Stage-specific overhead perspectives (Mode 7) suggest shifting environmental storytelling—from tactical side-scrolling combat to reconnaissance overhead views
Ending celebration sequences vary by difficulty: Normal adds child to crowd, Hard adds friendly dog variant, reflecting narrative weight of achievement

boss_design.philosophy: Bosses serve as stage climaxes with unique visual spectacle and mechanical patterns. Director Nakazato prioritized cinematic presentation; notable example is mid-stage hanging-from-flying-missiles sequence requiring custom raster scrolling programming to make background tiles appear as foreground sprites. Each of six stages features end boss; some stages have mid-stage bosses.
notable_bosses: Stage-specific bosses tied to alien invasion theme
True final boss (appears only on Normal/Hard modes, not Easy)

faction_indicators: Elite commando protagonists (Bill Rizer/Lance Bean or robots RD-008/RC-011 in EU) represent human/mechanized resistance
Alien invaders as monolithic threat across all regions
Regional character swap signals cultural compliance vs. artistic vision tension
danger_signals: Alien invasion framing establishes existential threat narrative
Escalating difficulty and final boss gate suggests humanity's survival hinges on player performance
Power-up loss mechanic creates tension: success/failure tied to resource conservation decisions
Limited continues (international versions) create permanent mission failure states
environmental_storytelling: Six stages progress across Earth locations (specifics not documented, but themed per stage)
Overhead Mode 7 stages shift perspective to reconnaissance/infiltration feel vs. direct combat
Stage-specific boss designs and environments contextualize alien stronghold diversity
Ending montage images (with timestamps in JP version, removed in international) show character progression through battle zones
